mechanism_narrative: Rising non-farm payroll employment signals expanding labor demand, which supports corporate earnings growth and boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This relationship holds continuously without a specific threshold activation.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of monthly change in PAYEMS versus 20-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling period, or the average return during months of increasing PAYEMS is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate reaches or falls below 0.25%, the zero lower bound signals extreme monetary accommodation. Such conditions compress discount rates and stimulate risk-taking, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: After the trigger date, the mean 5-day forward return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test vs zero, p > 0.05), or more than 60% of triggered instances produce negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Combined high unemployment (UNRATE above 8%) and rising industrial production (INDPRO increasing over 3 months) signal a jobless recovery where output recovers faster than labor markets. This imbalance suggests continued slack and potential for further growth without inflationary pressure, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 8% and INDPRO rising over 3 months
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return when both conditions are met is not significantly above the unconditional average return (t-test p > 0.10), or the Rank IC of the combined signal over rolling 3-year windows is < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle expansion phases, rising retail and food services sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and equity prices higher over the subsequent 20 trading days. This mechanism is most powerful in the early-cycle regime and may reverse in late-cycle.
failure_condition: In periods identified as early-cycle (based on NBER cycle or yield curve), the correlation between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (Pearson r < 0.10), or the proportion of positive returns during rising RSAFS months is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 10% signals a significant contraction in residential investment, which historically precedes economic downturns. Such declines predict negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days as the housing weakness spreads to broader economy.
failure_condition: Following HOUST drops >10%, the mean 60-day forward return is not negative (one-sided t-test vs zero, p > 0.10), or fewer than 50% of such events produce negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month increases in the CPI (CPIAUCSL) reduce real money balances and raise discount rates, negatively impacting equity valuations. This inflation signal continuously pressures S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as markets adjust to rising price levels.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between monthly CPI change and 5-day forward returns is not negative (rank IC > 0) over any 3-year period, or the average return during months of rising CPI is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) is positive and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is below 1%, the economy experiences strong demand with very accommodative monetary policy. This combination amplifies equity returns over the next 60 trading days as low rates boost present values of growing earnings.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth positive and FEDFUNDS < 1%
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return when both conditions are met is less than the unconditional average return, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-crisis recovery regime (characterized by low capacity utilization and previous financial shock), rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a normalization of manufacturing activity and supply chains. This signal strongly predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors price in recovery momentum.
failure_condition: During the post-crisis period (2009-2012), the correlation between monthly INDPRO growth and 20-day forward returns is not positive (r < 0.10), or the proportion of positive returns when INDPRO rises over three months is below 55%.
